#  Create a Purchase Order from an Estimate 
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/bid-board-company/permissions) for the **Bid Board** tool.
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/estimating-project/permissions) for the **Estimating** tool.
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/portfolio-planning-company/permissions) for the **Portfolio Planning** tool.
  * To send an estimate to the budget, the estimate must be set as the primary estimate as indicated with a checkmark ![Icon Green Checkmark](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/0b18333d194d3db8dbcc1b8a78cbdfec/8ff203a9339f899ef677a436aa111bdd/icon-green-checkmark.png?fm=webp&h=20) icon.
  * The estimate has to be sent to the budget before you can create a purchase order.


##### Note
To ensure process streamlining, clear accountability, and the prevention of potential discrepancies or overlaps, the Estimating section is authorized to issue a single Purchase Order (PO) and a single Prime Contract for each distinct estimate developed.
##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the **Bid Board** or **Portfolio Planning** tool and select the **project**.  
OR  
Navigate to the project's **Estimating** tool.
  2. Click the **Estimating** tab.
  3. Select the estimate.  

![Estimate Selection Menu](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/2a01a716f966b487b0d3d48145af3e2c/5081994b0555d96f62d8cb26890d5712/estimate-selection-menu.png?fm=webp)
  4. Click the **Actions** menu.  

![Estimate Actions Menu](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/98566424885fd092537f825d27c1e266/af1db3e010fd3a9221a14b6220b2745a/estimate-actions-menu.png?fm=webp)
  5. Select **Create Purchase Order**.
  6. Review the purchase order.
  7. Click **Send to Purchase Order** to create a purchase order in the Commitments tool.


## See Also
  * [Estimating - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/estimating-project/permissions)
  * [What gets copied over to a new project from a project template?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-gets-copied-over-to-a-new-project-when-applying-a-project-template)
  * [Add a Change Order for an Estimate](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/estimating-project/tutorials/add-a-change-order-for-an-estimate)
title: Create a Purchase Order from an Estimate
description: How to create a purchase order from an estimate in the Estimating, Bid Board, or Portfolio Planning tools.
